mechanism_narrative: Positive monthly momentum in retail sales reflects strengthening household demand that raises expected corporate revenues, so higher RSAFS growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest RSAFS snapshot is 465513 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of observations or the walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate tightens financial conditions and raises discount rates, so when FEDFUNDS crosses above its trailing 6-month average the S&P 500 produces negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its trailing 6-month average
evidence_refs: fact:latest FEDFUNDS snapshot is 0.41 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate combined with accelerating consumer prices signals late-cycle wage and margin pressure, so UNRATE below 5% with rising CPIAUCSL predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.0 and CPIAUCSL 3-month growth rate > its trailing 12-month growth rate
evidence_refs: fact:latest UNRATE snapshot is 4.6 as of t-1mo
fact:latest CPIAUCSL snapshot is 242.348 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between low UNRATE and accelerating CPIAUCSL has a non-negative coefficient in walk-forward return regressions or triggered 60-day returns exceed baseline returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts indicates stronger residential investment and future demand for durable goods, so HOUST crossing above its trailing 12-month average predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its trailing 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:latest HOUST snapshot is 1323 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakouts above the trailing 12-month average are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of instances or the mean triggered return is below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals weaker goods-sector output and lower operating leverage for cyclicals, so negative 3-month INDPRO momentum predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest INDPRO snapshot is 103.8589 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if observations with negative 3-month INDPRO momentum have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or show a walk-forward hit rate no better than the unconditional base rate.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer prices accelerate while policy rates rise, equity valuations face simultaneous inflation and discount-rate pressure, so rising CPIAUCSL together with rising FEDFUNDS predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth > 2.5 and FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS trigger produces non-negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if removing either proxy does not reduce out-of-sample predictive accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Above-trend real GDP growth indicates broad demand strength and supports earnings revisions, so GDPC1 year-over-year growth above its trailing 8-quarter median predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth > its trailing 8-quarter median
evidence_refs: fact:latest GDPC1 snapshot is 16712.5 as of t-5mo
failure_condition: Reject if above-median GDPC1 growth is followed by 60-day S&P 500 returns below the unconditional mean in more than 55% of historical real-time observations.

mechanism_narrative: A deterioration in employment conditions signals rising recession risk and weaker household income growth, so UNRATE rising sharply while PAYEMS growth slows predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 3 months and PAYEMS 3-month growth < its trailing 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:latest UNRATE snapshot is 4.6 as of t-1mo
fact:latest PAYEMS snapshot is 145128 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if this joint labor deterioration trigger is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the interaction coefficient is not negative in walk-forward regressions.
